## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about six decisions that you need to make before creating your webinar. Besides the product related ones, like what you're gonna sell for how much and how will that be delivered, they're these six key decisions. And each of those decisions is important in defining the nature of your webinar and getting the right people to attend your webinar and getting those who attend to be ready to buy your product when you make the offer on your webinar. And this is a key part of this particular module because we need to have these decisions made and thought through carefully before we start actually building out the rest of the funnel. So this is the strategy that precedes the webinar creation itself. I'm going to be talking about each one of these in separate videos, but I'm just going to introduce them here. The first one is what marketing you're targeting. In other words, what are the characteristics of the people that you want to attend your webinar because these are the same characteristics of the people who potentially will buy your product. Next is what are their biggest needs? What issues should you be resolving in the webinar? They're most likely to lead to a sell later on. So there are many issues that we can resolve in a webinar, but we want to choose ones that actually will result in them being prepared to buy from you when you make the offer in the webinar. Next, what transformation are you offering in the webinar? In other words, how will attending your webinar change their life? And this is separate from the transformation that you offer in your product, because if you offer the exact same transformation in your webinar that you're doing in your product, they say, well, he just got me to spend an hour and a half sitting in this webinar and didn't solve the problem. What makes me think he's going to solve that in this product that he's trying to sell me? Next, what marketing hook will you use to attract them into attending your webinar? What short phrase will you use to get them interested in attending your webinar? And I'll explain this in a lot more detail because I know a lot of people don't know exactly what a marketing hook is. Next, what are you actually going to present in your webinar? What type of content will you present, both to attract people and to prepare them to buy? And then number six, what tone are you going to use in your webinar? What character or attitude do you most want to reflect in your webinar? Because that can make a big difference

## [03:01 - 03:49]

in both how many people buy and whether the people who buy are people that you really want to attract into your business. Each of these decisions affects what your webinar is about, how you go about marketing it, what you talk about in each promotion you use to convince people to register and attend and watch the replay and buy, and how you word each of these promotions and how your webinar is actually structured. So since each of these decisions plays a role in your webinar and it's marketing, we're going to look at each one of them more specifically and make some decisions to influence everything else we do in this entire program.
